name: cal-com-automation
description: Automate Cal.com tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): manage bookings, check availability, configure webhooks, and handle teams. Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: List and filter bookings by status, date range, and other criteria
Create new bookings by finding available slots and submitting requests
Check calendar availability and busy time blocks for date ranges
Configure webhooks for booking events like creation, rescheduling, and cancellation
Manage teams including creation, retrieval, and team event types
Retrieve organization ID for org-level operations
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is a documentation file (SKILL.md) that describes how to use Cal.com automation via Rube MCP. All 64 static analyzer findings are false positives: markdown backticks used for code formatting were incorrectly flagged as Ruby shell execution, documentation URLs were flagged as network access, and parameter descriptions were flagged as environment access. No executable code is present in this file.
valueStatement: Managing Cal.com bookings manually is time-consuming. This skill automates booking management, availability checking, webhook configuration, and team operations through Rube MCP.
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP connection with Cal.com toolkit configured
Cannot bypass Cal.com API rate limits or authentication requirements
Team and organization operations require appropriate permissions
Does not work with personal Cal.com accounts that lack organization features

useCases.targetUser: Executive assistants and coordinators managing multiple schedules
useCases.title: Automated Booking Assistant
useCases.description: Help users find available meeting slots and create bookings without manual calendar checking.

useCases.targetUser: Developers integrating Cal.com with CRM or notification systems
useCases.title: Webhook Integration Setup
useCases.description: Configure Cal.com webhooks to trigger notifications in external systems when bookings change.

useCases.targetUser: Team leads and HR managers organizing team events
useCases.title: Team Scheduling Management
useCases.description: Create and manage team event types for group bookings and collaborative scheduling.

promptTemplates.title: View upcoming bookings
promptTemplates.prompt: List all my upcoming Cal.com bookings for the next two weeks.

promptTemplates.title: Find available meeting slots
promptTemplates.prompt: Check available slots for event type [ID] between [start date] and [end date] in [timezone].

promptTemplates.title: Create a new booking
promptTemplates.prompt: Book a meeting for [attendee name] at [email] on [date] at [time] for event type [ID].

promptTemplates.title: Set up booking notifications
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure a webhook to send notifications to [URL] when bookings are created or cancelled.

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before executing workflows
Verify Rube MCP connection status is ACTIVE before running any Cal.com operations
Specify timezone explicitly in all date/time parameters to avoid scheduling confusion
antiPatterns: Do not create bookings without first checking available slots for the event type
Do not use date ranges spanning months for availability checks as results may be inaccurate
Do not assume team or organization access - verify permissions before operations

faq.question: What do I need to set up before using this skill?
faq.answer: You need Rube MCP configured with an active Cal.com connection. Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server, then authenticate Cal.com through RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS.

faq.question: How do I find my event type ID?
faq.answer: Use CAL_GET_AVAILABLE_SLOTS_INFO or check your Cal.com dashboard. Event type IDs are numeric integers that identify specific meeting configurations.

faq.question: Can I use this with a personal Cal.com account?
faq.answer: Basic booking operations work with personal accounts. However, team and organization management features require a Cal.com organization plan.

faq.question: What date formats should I use?
faq.answer: Use ISO 8601 format with timezone for booking times (2024-01-15T09:00:00Z) and YYYY-MM-DD format for availability date ranges.

faq.question: How do webhook notifications work?
faq.answer: Webhooks send HTTP POST requests to your endpoint when specified events occur. You need a publicly accessible HTTPS URL to receive notifications.

faq.question: What happens if I hit Cal.com API rate limits?
faq.answer: Cal.com returns 429 status on rate limit. The skill should implement exponential backoff and retry after the specified delay.
